diagnostics.message: "chart.js" is a heavy library — use React.lazy() or next/dynamic for code splitting
diagnostics.help: Use `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic or React.lazy()

diagnostics.message: Avoid initializing state in an effect. Instead, initialize "patients"'s `useState()` with "undefined". For SSR hydration, prefer `useSyncExternalStore()`.
diagnostics.help: Pass the initial value directly to useState() instead of setting it from a mount-only useEffect. For SSR hydration, prefer useSyncExternalStore().

diagnostics.message: Three-period ellipsis ("...") in JSX text — use the actual ellipsis character "…" (or `…`)
diagnostics.help: Use the typographic ellipsis "…" (or `…`) instead of three periods — pairs with action-with-followup labels ("Rename…", "Loading…")

diagnostics.message: "react-chartjs-2" is a heavy library — use React.lazy() or next/dynamic for code splitting
diagnostics.help: Use `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic or React.lazy()

diagnostics.message: Avoid initializing state in an effect. Instead, initialize "patients"'s `useState()` with "undefined". For SSR hydration, prefer `useSyncExternalStore()`.

diagnostics.message: Component "VitalsEntry" has 6 useState calls — consider useReducer for related state
diagnostics.help: Group related state: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, { field1, field2, ... })`

diagnostics.message: useState "year" is updated but never read in the component's return — use useRef so updates don't trigger re-renders
diagnostics.help: Replace useState with useRef when the value is only mutated and never read in render — `ref.current = ...` updates without re-rendering the component

diagnostics.message: Avoid initializing state in an effect. Instead, initialize "year"'s `useState()` with "<expression>()". For SSR hydration, prefer `useSyncExternalStore()`.
diagnostics.help: Pass the initial value directly to useState() instead of setting it from a mount-only useEffect. For SSR hydration, prefer useSyncExternalStore().

diagnostics.message: useEffect(setState, []) on mount causes a flash — consider useSyncExternalStore or suppressHydrationWarning
diagnostics.help: Use `useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, getSnapshot, getServerSnapshot)` or add `suppressHydrationWarning` to the element

diagnostics.message: new Date() reachable from JSX renders differently on server vs client — wrap in useEffect+useState (client-only) or add suppressHydrationWarning to the parent if intentional
diagnostics.help: Wrap dynamic time/random values in useEffect+useState (client-only) or add suppressHydrationWarning to the parent if intentional

diagnostics.message: z-index: 1000 is arbitrarily high — use a deliberate z-index scale (1–50). Extreme values signal a stacking context problem, not a fix
diagnostics.help: Define a z-index scale in your design tokens (e.g. dropdown: 10, modal: 20, toast: 30). Create a new stacking context with `isolation: isolate` instead of escalating values

diagnostics.message: useContext is superseded by `use()` on React 19+ — `use()` reads context conditionally inside hooks, branches, and loops; switch to `import { use } from 'react'`
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a regular prop on function components — `forwardRef` is no longer needed in React 19+. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)` for branch-aware context reads. Only enabled on projects detected as React 19+.

diagnostics.message: Avoid storing derived state. Compute "statusText" directly during render, optionally with `useMemo` if it's expensive.
diagnostics.help: Compute derived state inline during render (or with useMemo if expensive) instead of mirroring it into useState via a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#updating-state-based-on-props-or-state

diagnostics.message: Derived state in useEffect — compute during render instead
diagnostics.help: For derived state, compute inline: `const x = fn(dep)`. For state resets on prop change, use a key prop: `<Component key={prop} />`. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect

diagnostics.message: 1500ms transition is too slow for UI feedback — keep transitions under 1000ms. Use longer durations only for page-load hero animations
diagnostics.help: Keep UI transitions under 1s — 100-150ms for instant feedback, 200-300ms for state changes, 300-500ms for layout changes. Use longer durations only for page-load hero animations

diagnostics.help: Use `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic or React.lazy()

diagnostics.message: Prefer the semantic `<button>` element over `role="button"` on a generic tag.
diagnostics.help: Replace `role` with the semantic HTML element when one exists.

diagnostics.message: Prefer the semantic `<button>` element over `role="button"` on a generic tag.
diagnostics.help: Replace `role` with the semantic HTML element when one exists.

diagnostics.message: useState "gps" is updated but never read in the component's return — use useRef so updates don't trigger re-renders
diagnostics.help: Replace useState with useRef when the value is only mutated and never read in render — `ref.current = ...` updates without re-rendering the component

diagnostics.message: Avoid storing derived state. Compute "statusText" directly during render, optionally with `useMemo` if it's expensive.
diagnostics.help: Compute derived state inline during render (or with useMemo if expensive) instead of mirroring it into useState via a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#updating-state-based-on-props-or-state

diagnostics.message: Derived state in useEffect — compute during render instead
diagnostics.help: For derived state, compute inline: `const x = fn(dep)`. For state resets on prop change, use a key prop: `<Component key={prop} />`. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect

diagnostics.message: Avoid initializing state in an effect. Instead, initialize "patient"'s `useState()` with "undefined". For SSR hydration, prefer `useSyncExternalStore()`.
diagnostics.help: Pass the initial value directly to useState() instead of setting it from a mount-only useEffect. For SSR hydration, prefer useSyncExternalStore().

diagnostics.message: 1500ms transition is too slow for UI feedback — keep transitions under 1000ms. Use longer durations only for page-load hero animations
diagnostics.help: Keep UI transitions under 1s — 100-150ms for instant feedback, 200-300ms for state changes, 300-500ms for layout changes. Use longer durations only for page-load hero animations
